memory-graph.customInstructions: Unified semantic memory for AI agents. MANDATORY at task start: call memory_primer() once to orient (top topics + key canonicals + coverage + store size, token-cheap), then recall(query=<task>, top_k=5, compact=true) before doing any work — it returns fused matches across memories+KG+wiki plus top_canonicals; compact=true dedupes and trims to save tokens, and group_topics=true clusters hits by theme. After solving: call record_finding(finding_type, title, content, related_files, tags, sources=[file:line/URL/commit]) to persist; this creates vector memory + KG node + auto-infers RELATED_TO edges + auto-crystallizes wiki on canonical promotion. sources ground the claim and keep memory_gaps from flagging it. Use wiki_get(canonical_id, outline_only=true) then wiki_get(canonical_id, section='…') to load only what you need. Use memory_map to see the topic mind map and memory_gaps to find coverage holes. For typed relationships (SOLVES, SUPERSEDES, DEPENDS_ON) use kg_add_edge. For multi-agent state use collective_store/get/list with TTL. For expensive idempotent calls use cache_check/cache_store. See PROTOCOL.md for the full operating manual.
